title: Golden Roasted Northern Irish Sausages Bangers And Mash
description: A hearty and indulgent British dish featuring roasted Northern Irish sausages, fluffy mashed potatoes, and sweet Bramley apples, all made without onions or garlic. This comforting meal is perfect for a cold evening.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Place the sausages on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and roast for 20 minutes.
While the sausages are cooking, boil the chopped potatoes in a large pot of salted water until they are tender.
Drain the potatoes and mash them with butter, milk, salt, and pepper in a large bowl.
In a mortar and pestle, pound the chopped Bramley apples until they are well broken down.
Add the pounded apples to the mashed potatoes and stir until well combined.
Serve the roasted sausages on top of the apple and potato mash, garnished with chopped fresh parsley.
Return the dish to the oven for an additional 5 minutes to heat through.
Remove the dish from the oven and serve hot.
